title: Sunhigh
desc:
The traditional festival of summer solstice, oft called the Midsummer Feast by commoners. On the first dae, the local priests and notables oft prepare alms with the latest fruits and food of summer.

In Azuria, the commonly served foods includes: strawberries, blackberries and raspberries, served with cold sliced cucumber. Plums and tangerine, draped with fresh honey. Fresh bread of new summer wheat, oft served with lamb or mutton, eels and river fishes in stew or pan fried. Salmon are most popular, for they return from Abyssor's seas to spawn in the rivers of Azuria, and are the fattest and most bountiful in this season. Minced salmon pies, fried salmon, salmon steaks - everything is served with salmon. The lucky or rich one will have raw fatty salmon in the style, methodically prepared and chilled by Magos for safety, then served with a dip of fermented fish sauce in traditional Azurian style and perhaps with honey and berries, though such meals tend to disappear surprisingly quickly when a druid is involved in the feast. Honeycakes are oft served to the nobles, in Ranesheni style with added sugar. And of course, cheeses of all kinds on rich platter.

On the second dae, a statue of Astrata is borne upon a palanquin from her church to the city's administrative center, and then symbolically marched through its many streets in a circuit by the assembled clergy before returning to its main church. The statue is wreathed in flowers along the way, and wishes are placed upon it - the nobles getting first pick during the procession. Then it is marched unto the church, the wishes are stored in a box to be burnt at Sunwake come next midwinter, and the statue properly reseated in its place.

On the third dae, a reverse circuit begins with a statue or symbol of Noc, who is paraded through and then set in front of the seat of power, as Astrata herself will be paraded back at Sunwake come midwinter. A second feast is oft held, smaller than the first dae's, and consists of simple, fatty fried meat and breads, to fill one's stomach up for the autumn and winter to come.

title: Sunwake
desc:
The festival of winter solstice, celebrated in Astrata's name. On the first dae, the statue of Noc is covered in a shroud and then taken away in the middle of the night to the Church - known as the night procession. The local libraries and places of learning open their doors, and the teachers and the literate give stories of Noc's many blessings, knowledge, and magicks to any who would listen, oft serving simple breads with fruit jams and ham as refreshment.

On the second dae, a long series of festivals begins, starting with a carrying of Astrata's statue back to the seat of power, veiled in a shroud, before being revealed in much fanfare. Then, at the very spot the statue lays, beggars and nobles alike are invited to a great feast hosted by the Church and the notables, though heavy snow oft gets in the way and it is held indoors nearby. Delicious, hearty Azurian stew is served with potatoes, breadpieces and salted pork. An overabundance of roasted, grilled and stewed river fishes and sea fishes, for Abyssor's bounty does not end with the season, unlike Dendor's.

In the section reserved for the nobles, they oft feast on games hunted in the forests of Azuria, taken from their nests in winter by hunters trudging through snow for the remaining fattened animals - and when they all come back lean, the household chefs mince them with fat and butter preserved from autumn to make indulgent meatpies for the well to do. All these are served with dried tangerines, fruits, and jams preserved in the autumn. The wishes from Sunhigh are burnt publicly in front of the statue, and it is said those whose smoke rises the highest will be heard by Astrata herself - and even a commoner might find themselves elevated to nobility.

Throughout the great feast, choirs and songs of praising are sung, in glory of Noc and Astrata. The feast extends into the third and last dae, and ends only when a pinch of the last harvest's wheat is thrown into the hearth at the dusk of the third dae. And thus everyone returns to home, their bellies filled and spirits high, to spend their time with their family, to look forward to the coming of Astrata again, and the end of the longest nights.
